audience: Chinese LLM application developers and technical readers
purpose: Explain Anthropic's practical guidance for building effective LLM agents and workflows, with emphasis on simple composable patterns over complex frameworks.
thesis: Effective agentic systems usually start from simple augmented LLM building blocks and only add workflow or agent complexity when evaluation shows that the added complexity improves outcomes.
structure: Definition and scope of agents versus workflows
When to use agents and when not to add complexity
Framework guidance and augmented LLM building block
Guidance for combining patterns and applying core principles
Appendices on customer support, coding agents, and tool prompt engineering
risks: Keep product names and model names unchanged.
Preserve image references and link targets exactly.
Distinguish tools as a general concept from tool use as an API capability.
Retain the author's caution against unnecessary complexity.
Preserve emphasis and Markdown structure in exported Chinese Markdown.
reference_policy: Keep URLs, product names, model names, benchmark names, code-like terms, and Markdown image paths unchanged unless a glossary entry explicitly says otherwise.
quality_bar: Publication-ready Chinese Markdown that is faithful to source logic, clear to technical readers, structurally aligned with the original, and free of unresolved medium+ blocking issues.
